metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the exact Gerrit account login or group name (e.g., `autosubmit-bot` service user or `label-Autosubmit-voters` group) that receives the vote ACL in AC2, so the executor can write the project.config stanza without ambiguity.
findings.finding: The bot account / Gerrit group that receives the `label-Autosubmit` vote ACL is referred to only as 'the auto-lander bot account' without naming the actual account or group. An executor writing the ACL stanza in project.config cannot proceed without this name.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria – AC2: 'label-Autosubmit vote ACL restricted to the auto-lander bot account (+ Administrators)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC2: 'Restricted to Gerrit service account `autosubmit-bot` (group `Autosubmit Voters`) and `Administrators`; stanza: `[access "refs/heads/*"] label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Autosubmit Voters`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reframe AC5 as a concrete, deterministic check: e.g., assert via `GET /projects/rebar/access` that `LLM-Review` and `Verified` submit requirements are unchanged, and that `Autosubmit` does not appear in any `submitRequirement` block of project.config (command: `grep -c 'Autosubmit' infra/gerrit/project.config submit-requirements` asserting 0 matches).
findings.finding: AC5 ('Security review: the label cannot weaken the two-vote gate') is process-framed ('security review') rather than a specific observable outcome, and has no concrete proving command; it cannot be evaluated deterministically in-session.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria – AC5: 'Security review: the label cannot weaken the two-vote (LLM-Review+Verified) gate'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace AC5 with: 'Verify no submit requirement references `Autosubmit` (`grep -E "submittable|submitRequirement" infra/gerrit/project.config` returns no line containing `Autosubmit`), and `GET /projects/rebar/access` shows `LLM-Review` and `Verified` submit requirements unchanged from pre-cutover snapshot.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the exact Gerrit group for label-Autosubmit (e.g. `Service Users` or a new dedicated group) and, if it is a new group, enumerate `setup-project.sh` as an edit target requiring a new group-creation block and an update to the `want=` dictionary (line 123).
findings.finding: setup-project.sh contains a hardcoded `want=` group-UUID dictionary (line 123) that must include every group referenced by project.config's ACL stanzas, or the refs/meta/config push fails with 'unknown group'. The plan's AC2 says label-Autosubmit is restricted to 'the auto-lander bot account' but does not name the actual Gerrit account or group. If the ACL references a new group (not the existing 'Service Users'), setup-project.sh needs both a new group-creation block (analogous to the Contributors block) AND an update to the `want=` dictionary — but neither edit is enumerated in the plan.
findings.location: AC2 (label-Autosubmit vote ACL); infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh line 123
findings.suggested_fix: In the plan's 'What' or AC2, resolve the group name: if the auto-lander bot account is already in `Service Users`, state `label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Service Users` and note no script change is needed. If a dedicated group is required, add setup-project.sh to the edit set with the same group-creation + want= pattern used for `Contributors`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the plan which Gerrit group backs `label-Autosubmit` (existing group already in `setup-project.sh`'s `want` dict, or a new group) and, if new, add the required `create-group` + `want` dict update to the cutover steps so the `refs/meta/config` push is not rejected with 'unknown group'.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify which Gerrit group backs the `label-Autosubmit` ACL, leaving a hidden ordering dependency: if the ACL references a NEW named group not already in `setup-project.sh`'s hard-coded `want` dict (line 123), Gerrit will reject the `refs/meta/config` push with an 'unknown group' error — the exact failure mode the codebase explicitly documents. The plan must either (a) name an existing group (`Service Users`, `Contributors`) or (b) specify that `setup-project.sh` is updated with a new group-create step and a `want` entry before the cutover push.
findings.location: AC2 ('label-Autosubmit vote ACL restricted to the auto-lander bot account'); infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh line 123
findings.suggested_fix: If the auto-lander bot already belongs to `Service Users`, reuse that group and note it explicitly (no `setup-project.sh` change needed). If a dedicated group is preferred (tighter ACL), add a `create-group auto-lander` step to `setup-project.sh` (mirroring the `Contributors` block, lines 91–107) and add `["auto-lander"]=1` to the `want` dict at line 123.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit `value =` string definitions for all three Autosubmit values to AC1 (e.g., `value = -1 Do not land`, `value = 0 No request`, `value = +1 Please land`) so the label schema is fully specified as a machine-readable enum before the consuming-service story is written.
findings.finding: The `Autosubmit` label's value vocabulary (`value =` strings) is never defined in the plan. AC1 states '-1..+1' and 'function = NoBlock' but does not specify the named value lines (e.g., `value = -1 Do not land`, `value = 0 No score`, `value = +1 Land this change`). Every existing label in infra/gerrit/project.config (LLM-Review lines 105-107, Verified lines 139-141) carries explicit `value =` string definitions. The consuming bot story (explicitly deferred) will reference this label's schema; without value strings the definition is structurally incomplete as an agent-readable enum.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1: '`Autosubmit` label added to `infra/gerrit/project.config` (`-1..+1`, `function = NoBlock`, empty `copyCondition`…)'
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC1 to read: '`Autosubmit` label added to `infra/gerrit/project.config` (`-1..+1`, `function = NoBlock`, empty `copyCondition`) with value strings: `value = -1 Do not land / value = 0 No autosubmit request / value = +1 Please land this change`.' This matches the vocabulary pattern of the existing labels and gives the consuming-bot story a canonical enum reference.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for AC2 that actually reads ACL grants, e.g., `curl -s '.../projects/rebar/access' | jq -e '.local["refs/heads/*"].permissions["label-Autosubmit"]'` and assert it contains only the bot group/Administrators, to verify the forge-prevention claim.
findings.finding: AC2 claims the vote ACL is restricted to the bot, and that ordinary users and `addPatchSet` cannot forge it, but the named proving command (`GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit`) returns label metadata only — it does NOT expose ACL grants, so the ACL restriction is never concretely verified.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria – AC2 and AC4: 'verified via GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the AC4 proving command for ACL verification with: `curl -s 'https://<gerrit>/a/projects/rebar/access' | jq -e '.local["refs/heads/*"].permissions["label-Autosubmit"].rules | keys == ["<bot-group-id>", "Administrators"]'` — this exercises the actual ACL, not just the label definition.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Strengthen AC5 by adding an explicit verification step: diff the `[submit-requirements]` and `[label 'LLM-Review']`/`[label 'Verified']` stanzas before and after the cutover and assert they are unchanged (e.g., `git diff refs/meta/config~1 refs/meta/config -- project.config | grep -E 'submit-requirements|label-LLM-Review|label-Verified'`).
findings.finding: AC5 declares a security property ('the label cannot weaken the two-vote gate') but specifies no concrete verification method or evidence criteria, leaving the cutover gate-integrity check undeclared and potentially unverifiable at review time.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC5: 'Security review: the label cannot weaken the two-vote (`LLM-Review`+`Verified`) gate.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC5: 'Verified by: (a) `git diff` of `refs/meta/config` confirms zero changes to `[submit-requirements]`, `[label "LLM-Review"]`, and `[label "Verified"]` stanzas; (b) `GET /projects/rebar/submit_requirements` returns the same set as pre-cutover.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the exact Gerrit group that receives the `label-Autosubmit` ACL in the plan, verify whether it already exists in `setup-project.sh`'s `want` dict (line 123), and explicitly scope any required `setup-project.sh` update into this story.
findings.finding: The plan's operator-cutover path implicitly relies on `setup-project.sh` as the push tool (the established pattern for all prior label additions), but `setup-project.sh:123` has a hardcoded `want` dict of exactly four groups (Administrators, Service Users, feature-branch-drivers, Contributors). The plan names the ACL recipient only as 'the auto-lander bot account' without specifying whether this reuses an existing group (e.g., Service Users) or introduces a new Gerrit group. If a new group is introduced, `setup-project.sh` must be updated — but the plan scopes the story to 'Config + ACL + cutover ONLY' without acknowledging this dependency, making the cutover path incomplete under any new-group scenario.
findings.location: Scope / AC2 / What section ('operator cutover'); infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh:123
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence to the What/Scope section: 'The `label-Autosubmit` vote ACL is granted to group `<name>` (reusing Service Users / a new dedicated group — specify which); if a new group, extend `setup-project.sh:123`'s `want` dict and create the group before the cutover push.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] AC2: Name the exact Gerrit group (or account) for the `label-Autosubmit` vote ACL, and specify whether setup-project.sh's `want` dict and group-creation block must be updated to include it.
findings.finding: The plan never names the specific Gerrit group (or account) that will receive the `label-Autosubmit` vote ACL — AC2 only says 'the auto-lander bot account (+ Administrators)'. The existing setup-project.sh `want` dict (line 123) must include every group referenced in project.config so Gerrit can populate the `groups` file; an unnamed/uncreated group causes the refs/meta/config push to fail with 'unknown group'. Without knowing whether this is a new group (e.g. 'Auto-Landers'), an existing one ('Service Users'), or a direct account grant, the ACL cannot be implemented at minimum privilege.
findings.location: AC2: 'label-Autosubmit vote ACL restricted to the auto-lander bot account (+ Administrators)'
findings.suggested_fix: Either reuse the existing 'Service Users' group (and document that intent explicitly) OR add an 'Auto-Landers' group creation block to setup-project.sh (mirroring the Contributors block, lines 91-107) and add `["Auto-Landers"]=1` to the want dict on line 123. The plan should state which is intended.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm the exact Gerrit 3.14.1 REST path for single-label inspection (e.g., `GET /a/projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit` vs `GET /a/projects/rebar/labels?labelName=Autosubmit`), state the required auth credential, and add a concrete `curl` invocation (mirroring the smoke-check.sh pattern) to AC4 so the verification step is unambiguous.
findings.finding: AC4 specifies `GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit` as the post-cutover verification oracle, but this specific REST endpoint surface has not been used anywhere in the codebase. The Gerrit REST labels API path (`/projects/{name}/labels/{labelName}`) is a distinct surface from the already-used `/projects/{name}/config` endpoint. The plan does not state whether the authenticated `/a/` prefix is required, what credential to use, or what response shape to expect — leaving the verification step Partially-verified and potentially silently broken if the endpoint returns 404, 403, or a different path on 3.14.1.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4: 'verified via GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the bare endpoint reference with a concrete curl pattern that mirrors the existing smoke-check.sh convention: `curl -sS -u "${BOT_USER}:${TOKEN}" "https://${GERRIT_HOST}/a/projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit"` and assert the response contains `"name": "Autosubmit"`. If that path 404s on 3.14.1, fall back to `GET /a/projects/rebar/config` (already verified in evidence docs) and inspect the `labels` key.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add or reference a new ADR (or CLAUDE.md / design-doc section) that captures the `Autosubmit` label as an architectural decision: its opt-in lease semantics, non-gating rationale (FFO sufficiency), bot-only ACL policy, and the interface contract the future consuming service will depend on.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel architectural contract — the `Autosubmit` label as a machine-readable opt-in 'land intent' lease between agents and the auto-lander bot — but does not produce or reference a new ADR or design-doc entry capturing this decision, its rationale (non-gating, bot-only ACL, lease semantics), and the interface contract that the future consuming service will depend on.
findings.location: ## Why / ## What / Acceptance Criteria
findings.suggested_fix: Create ADR-XXXX (e.g., ADR-0041) titled 'Autosubmit label as opt-in land-intent lease' covering: (1) problem statement and why FFO makes gating redundant, (2) label parameters and why each was chosen, (3) bot-only ACL rationale and how to extend it safely, (4) interface contract for the consuming service (what voting +1 means, when it is cleared). Reference this ADR from the plan's Acceptance Criteria.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a CI lint/assertion step (e.g., in config-check) that validates Autosubmit label presence, function=NoBlock, and empty copyCondition on every future refs/meta/config change.
findings.finding: No ongoing observability is specified for the Autosubmit label state after cutover: there is no metric, alert, or log that would notify operators if the label configuration is accidentally mutated or removed in a future config change.
findings.location: AC: 'config-check passes; live cutover applied to refs/meta/config and verified via GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define a RETIRE path: specify how the Autosubmit label will be safely removed (e.g., drain all votes via bot, then remove config entry), and where this procedure is documented.
findings.finding: The RETIRE lifecycle path for the Autosubmit label is undefined: the plan provides no guidance on how to safely remove the label when it is eventually decommissioned, despite Gerrit retaining historical vote data on changes.
findings.location: ## Scope / overall plan — no retirement section present

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit interface note (e.g., in ## Scope or a linked ADR) stating that consumers MUST tolerate the label being absent or returning no votes, and that this is a precondition for the consumer story's AC.
findings.finding: The plan explicitly defers all consumers to a separate story but does not specify a contract requiring the consumer story to handle label-absent / no-votes-present gracefully, leaving a lifecycle gap if the consumer is deployed before or after the label.
findings.location: ## Scope: 'The service that reads the label is a separate story'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a least-privilege declaration for the bot account: state explicitly which Gerrit capabilities and label grants it holds (or will hold after this story), and confirm it does NOT hold `label-Code-Review+2`, `Submit`, or `Forge Author` grants.
findings.finding: The plan introduces the auto-lander bot account as the sole non-admin authorized voter on `label-Autosubmit` — a high-value trust position — but does not declare or bound the bot account's overall Gerrit permission profile, leaving its least-privilege posture undeclared.
findings.location: What / Acceptance Criteria — AC2: '`label-Autosubmit` vote ACL restricted to the auto-lander bot account (+ Administrators)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Security Review AC or a new AC: 'Bot account permission audit: the auto-lander service account holds ONLY `label-Autosubmit -1..+1`; it does not hold Submit, Code-Review+2, Forge-Author, or any capability that could unilaterally satisfy the LLM-Review+Verified gate.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the apparent contradiction between 'detect-only under ADR-0026' (What) and 'live cutover applied' (AC 4): either explain that ADR-0026 detect-only refers to the config-management bot's mode (not the operator action), or reconcile the two sections so the implementer has a single, unambiguous instruction.
findings.finding: The What section says the cutover is 'detect-only under ADR-0026', but AC 4 says 'live cutover applied to refs/meta/config and verified via GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit.' An implementer reading both sections faces contradictory instructions: apply the change live, or only detect drift.
findings.location: What section ('operator cutover — detect-only under ADR-0026') vs. AC 4 ('live cutover applied to refs/meta/config')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a parenthetical or note such as: 'ADR-0026 puts the config-management bot in detect-only mode, so the operator must push the change manually; this manual push IS the live cutover referenced in AC 4.' This eliminates the apparent contradiction.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether 'agents' (Why) and 'the auto-lander bot account' (AC 2) refer to the same Gerrit service account, and if not, list every account that needs `label-Autosubmit` vote permission so the ACL in AC 2 is complete and unambiguous.
findings.finding: The Why section states 'agents set and the bot consumes' the label, implying agents (likely automated CI/CD actors distinct from the auto-lander bot) need `label-Autosubmit` vote permission. But AC 2 restricts the vote ACL to 'the auto-lander bot account (+ Administrators).' If 'agents' and 'the auto-lander bot' are different accounts, agents cannot vote and the intent in Why cannot be fulfilled.
findings.location: Why section ('agents set and the bot consumes as a lease') vs. AC 2 ('label-Autosubmit vote ACL restricted to the auto-lander bot account (+ Administrators)')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback procedure for the refs/meta/config cutover (e.g., 'revert the label/ACL stanza and push to refs/meta/config; verify via GET endpoint that the label is absent'), including who is authorized to execute it and under what condition.
findings.finding: The plan specifies a live, operator-applied push to refs/meta/config (AC 4: 'live cutover applied') but provides no rollback or back-out procedure if the change causes unexpected breakage (e.g., ACL misconfiguration that locks out contributors).
findings.location: AC 4 / What section ('apply it to live refs/meta/config')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' subsection: 'If verification fails, revert by removing the `[label "Autosubmit"]` and corresponding `[access]` stanzas and pushing the prior HEAD of refs/meta/config. Confirm via GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit returning 404.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a rollback procedure to the cutover steps: record the current refs/meta/config HEAD SHA before the push, and document the `git push` revert command to restore it if post-cutover verification fails.
findings.finding: The plan specifies no rollback procedure for the live refs/meta/config cutover: if the pushed config produces unexpected Gerrit behavior (e.g., label misconfiguration breaks submit rules or Gerrit fails to load the config), there is no documented recovery path.
findings.location: AC: 'live cutover applied to refs/meta/config and verified via GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit'
findings.suggested_fix: Before cutover: `git ls-remote gerrit refs/meta/config` to capture current HEAD. After verification failure: `git push gerrit <saved-sha>:refs/meta/config --force` to restore prior state.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `infra/runbooks/two-vote-gate-rollback.md` (or a new companion runbook) to the edit set with a section describing how to disable/remove the `Autosubmit` label and revoke the bot's vote ACL.
findings.finding: infra/runbooks/two-vote-gate-rollback.md is an unenumerated consumer: it is the documented operator control surface for label/ACL changes to project.config and explicitly covers rollback of the existing two-vote gate. The new Autosubmit label needs an analogous rollback path (how to disable or revoke the label if the auto-lander misbehaves), but the runbook is not listed as an edit target.
findings.location: Scope section; infra/runbooks/two-vote-gate-rollback.md

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief prior-art note (Why or AC) acknowledging the Chromium/AOSP `Autosubmit` label as the canonical prior art for this pattern, and explicitly confirm that rebar's choices (range, copyCondition, ACL) are intentional alignments with or deliberate divergences from that reference implementation.
findings.finding: The plan introduces an `Autosubmit` Gerrit label with `function = NoBlock`, empty `copyCondition`, and a bot-only vote ACL — which is architecturally identical to the established Chromium/AOSP `Autosubmit` label pattern — but does not cite or cross-check this prior art. ADR-0040 already references 'the Android/Chromium/Go lineage' for the FFO commit-queue idiom, so the label's design choices (range `-1..+1`, empty copyCondition, bot-only ACL) should be validated against the Chromium canonical implementation to confirm intentional alignment or intentional divergence.
findings.location: Why / What sections; AC line 1 (label definition)
findings.suggested_fix: In the Why section, add: 'Prior art: this is the established Chromium/AOSP `Autosubmit`-label pattern (identical name, `NoBlock`, bot-only ACL). Rebar diverges in [X] because [reason], and aligns in [Y].'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
